title: Identify Tipper Components on a Left or Right Tipper
summary: Use Figure 3-4 and Table 3-3 from the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual to identify major tipper components on a left tipper and apply the same component naming to a right tipper, which the source states is a mirror-image assembly from the operator perspective.
when_to_use: Use when a technician or support user needs to identify named tipper components on the assembly, confirm left-versus-right tipper orientation from the operator perspective, or communicate component locations using the source-provided names from Figure 3-4 and Table 3-3.
not_for: Do not use for maintenance, adjustment, removal, replacement, or commissioning steps because this source packet only supports component identification and orientation reference.
Do not use to assign component names not listed in the source-provided figure and table.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Identify tipper orientation, use the figure and table to match component names, and record observed component locations using source-provided terminology.

safety_notes: This runbook is limited to identification and reference activity only; the packet does not provide source-supported maintenance or adjustment actions.
Do not infer removal, motion, or service actions from the component diagram alone.

access_required: Access to the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual figure and table referenced in this packet
system_preconditions: The tipper assembly to be identified is available for visual inspection.

visual_references.description: Primary labeled maintenance diagram for identifying major components on the left tipper assembly.

steps.title: Determine left or right tipper orientation
steps.purpose: Establish the correct orientation basis before using the left tipper figure for component identification.
steps.instruction: Determine whether the unit being checked is a left tipper or right tipper using the operator perspective described in the source.
steps.expected_result: The assembly is classified as either left tipper or right tipper from the operator perspective.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper side is identified consistently with the source statement that left and right are defined from the operator perspective.
steps.failure_condition: The observed assembly orientation cannot be confidently classified as left or right from the operator perspective.

actions.label: Identify tipper side
actions.exact_action: Determine whether the unit being checked is a left tipper or right tipper using the operator perspective described in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the left tipper reference view while applying the source note that left and right are defined from the operator perspective.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed assembly does not match the documented mirror-image expectation for left versus right tippers.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Right tippers and left tippers are based on the view from the perspective of the operator. The tippers are identical but assembled as mirror images of each other.

steps.title: Use the left tipper figure as the identification reference
steps.purpose: Anchor component identification to the source-provided labeled figure.
steps.instruction: Use Figure 3-4, which shows a left tipper, as the component identification reference.
steps.expected_result: The user is working from the correct labeled source figure for component identification.
steps.healthy_condition: Figure 3-4 is available and clearly used as the reference image for the left tipper assembly.
steps.failure_condition: The figure is unavailable, unclear, or not being used as the reference for identification.

actions.label: Open Figure 3-4 reference
actions.exact_action: Use Figure 3-4, which shows a left tipper, as the component identification reference.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Labeled Figure 3-4 showing the left tipper and numbered component callouts.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a required component cannot be confidently matched to the figure callout and table entry.

steps.title: Match figure callouts to the component table
steps.purpose: Identify the documented component names by matching the numbered figure callouts to the source table.
steps.instruction: Match the numbered callouts in the figure to Table 3-3 to identify the documented components: upper hard stops, Z-axis motor, Z-axis cable carrier, A-axis motor, lower hard stops, CLINK controller cover, column, sub-plate, counterbalances, counterbalance carriage, timing belt, A-axis cable carrier, air valve connection junction box, and gripper.
steps.expected_result: The selected callout is matched to a source-supported component name from Table 3-3.
steps.healthy_condition: Each observed component is identified using one of the source-provided names from Figure 3-4 and Table 3-3.
steps.failure_condition: A component cannot be confidently matched to a figure callout and corresponding table entry.

actions.label: Match callout to table entry
actions.exact_action: Match the numbered callouts in the figure to Table 3-3 to identify the documented components: upper hard stops, Z-axis motor, Z-axis cable carrier, A-axis motor, lower hard stops, CLINK controller cover, column, sub-plate, counterbalances, counterbalance carriage, timing belt, A-axis cable carrier, air valve connection junction box, and gripper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Maintenance diagram identifying the major components of a left-side OptiSweep tipper assembly and its numbered callouts.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a required component cannot be confidently matched to the figure callout and table entry.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 3-3 lists components including upper hard stops, Z-axis motor, Z-axis cable carrier, A-axis motor, lower hard stops, CLINK controller cover, column, sub-plate, counterbalances, counterbalance carriage, timing belt, A-axis cable carrier, air valve connection junction box, and gripper.

steps.title: Apply mirror-image mapping for a right tipper
steps.purpose: Use the source note about mirrored assembly orientation to identify the same components on a right tipper.
steps.instruction: If checking a right tipper, apply the source note that the assemblies are mirror images of each other and verify the same component names on the mirrored assembly orientation.
steps.expected_result: The same documented component names are identified on the right tipper using mirrored orientation relative to the left tipper figure.
steps.healthy_condition: The right tipper components correspond to the same source-provided names as the left tipper figure, with mirrored physical orientation.
steps.failure_condition: The right tipper does not appear to match the documented mirror-image relationship.

actions.label: Verify mirrored right tipper layout
actions.exact_action: If checking a right tipper, apply the source note that the assemblies are mirror images of each other and verify the same component names on the mirrored assembly orientation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the left tipper component layout as the baseline and verify the right tipper as a mirrored assembly.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed assembly does not match the documented mirror-image expectation for left versus right tippers.
Escalate if a required component cannot be confidently matched to the figure callout and table entry.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Right tippers and left tippers are based on the view from the perspective of the operator. The tippers are identical but assembled as mirror images of each other.

steps.title: Record the identified component and location
steps.purpose: Capture the identification result using only source-supported component names.
steps.instruction: Record the component name and observed location for the part being identified, using only the source-provided component names.
steps.expected_result: The identified part is documented with a source-supported name and observed location.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded component name matches one of the names listed in the source and the observed location is noted.
steps.failure_condition: The component cannot be recorded using a source-supported name with confidence.

actions.label: Document identified component
actions.exact_action: Record the component name and observed location for the part being identified, using only the source-provided component names.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a required component cannot be confidently matched to the figure callout and table entry.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 3-3 lists components including upper hard stops, Z-axis motor, Z-axis cable carrier, A-axis motor, lower hard stops, CLINK controller cover, column, sub-plate, counterbalances, counterbalance carriage, timing belt, A-axis cable carrier, air valve connection junction box, and gripper.

success_criteria: The user correctly identifies the documented tipper components on a left tipper using Figure 3-4 and Table 3-3.
The user applies the same component mapping to a right tipper as a mirror-image assembly from the operator perspective.
Recorded component names use only source-provided terminology.
healthy_conditions: Left and right tipper orientation is interpreted from the operator perspective.
The observed assembly matches the source statement that left and right tippers are mirror images of each other.
Components can be matched to the documented names in Figure 3-4 and Table 3-3.
failure_conditions: The observed assembly does not match the documented mirror-image expectation for left versus right tippers.
A required component cannot be confidently matched to the figure callout and table entry.
The user must rely on names or interpretations not supported by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed assembly does not match the documented mirror-image expectation for left versus right tippers.
Escalate if a required component cannot be confidently matched to the figure callout and table entry.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary source image for left tipper component callouts and mirrored right tipper interpretation.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Right tippers and left tippers are based on the view from the perspective of the operator. The tippers are identical but assembled as mirror images of each other.

Table 3-3 lists components including upper hard stops, Z-axis motor, Z-axis cable carrier, A-axis motor, lower hard stops, CLINK controller cover, column, sub-plate, counterbalances, counterbalance carriage, timing belt, A-axis cable carrier, air valve connection junction box, and gripper.

missing_details: The packet does not provide the full Table 3-3 callout numbering, so this runbook identifies components by source-provided names rather than explicit callout-number mappings.
The packet does not provide a source-supported estimate for completion time.
The packet does not provide source-supported requirements for production stop or LOTO for this identification-only activity.
The packet does not provide a source-supported recording format or destination for documenting identified component locations.
